title: triticum murale
side_effects: Triticum murale can have side effects if used improperly, such as allergic reactions, skin irritation, and respiratory issues in some individuals. Ingesting large quantities of the plant can lead to nausea, vomiting, and diarrhea due to its toxic compounds. Improper preparation or overuse of the plant can also cause headaches, dizziness, and fatigue. Furthermore, individuals with pre-existing medical conditions, such as kidney or liver disease, may experience exacerbated symptoms when using Triticum murale without proper guidance.
